data.title: China gives Detroit's Big 3 access to rare-earth minerals - report
data.description:
China has reversed its decision to restrict the export of rare earth minerals, giving Detroit’s Big 3 access to critical minerals used in the manufacture of electric vehicles and catalytic converters.

The decision to open the transfer of rare-earth minerals to U.S. automakers’ suppliers comes in the wake of a phone call between President Trump and Chinese president Xi Jinping on Thursday to de-escalate the simmering trade tensions between the two countries.

“There should no longer be any questions respecting the complexity of rare earth products,” Trump posted on social media.

China currently controls ~60% of the production of the rare earth supply and ~85% of the refined supply, giving the country enormous leverage over production of everything from smart phones, to electric vehicles.

And the country's control over the supply of these minerals has less to do with geographical abundance than China’s willingness to bear the high environmental cost in mining and refining of rare-earth minerals.

Without any alternative sources, manufacturers were exploring options like recovering existing stockpiles, and extracting oxides from recycled products like fluorescent lightbulbs.

After being cut-off since April China has issued a license to suppliers of General Motors (NYSE:GM), Ford (NYSE:F) and Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) to export refined minerals, enabling production to resume on vehicles like Ford’s (NYSE:F) Explorer SUV.

data.title: U.S. stock market could come back to double-digit returns in 2025 - Carson’s Detrick
data.description:
The U.S. stock market has rebounded significantly from April's woes, with the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) +19.20% from that month's closing low. Carson Group's Ryan Detrick believes this "early low" is evidence that the year can still end strongly and even offer up positive returns.

U.S. equities tanked in April after President Donald Trump's imposition of the steepest American trade barriers in over a century.

In the aftermath of Trump's April 2 announcement, the S&P 500 (SP500) swiftly entered into correction territory, and on April 7 briefly slipped into bear market territory. It notched its lowest closing level of the year on April 8 at 4,982.77 points, -15.28% YTD at that point.

"Another reminder that it is possible to be down 15% YTD at some point and come back to up double digits," Detrick said on X (formerly Twitter) on Friday.  

"The key of course is an early low, like we saw 2009 and 2020, when both bottomed in March," he added. 

See the chart he shared below:  

This year could see a turnaround much like 2009, a year after the 2008 financial crisis, and 2020, the COVID-19 crash. In both years, the markets had fallen more than 15% earlier in the year but then eventually ended up rallying in double digits. 

"2025 bottomed in April and the slingshot effect is in full motion," Detrick said.

On Friday, the S&P 500 (SP500) surpassed the 6,000 points mark for the first time since late February, though it had climbed back from that level. The benchmark gauge has gained +1.9% YTD. Investors can track the index through ETFs including (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:SSO) and (NYSEARCA:SH).

data.title: Proxy firms weigh in on the board battle at PENN Entertainment
data.description:
PENN Entertainment (NASDAQ:PENN) has a key shareholder meeting set for June 17. Shareholders will decide if they want three independent director candidates nominated by HG Vora Capital Management to join the company's board of Directors. 

The vote follows sharp criticism of Penn's (NASDAQ:PENN) recent performance, particularly regarding its interactive strategy and management oversight. HG Vora said its trio of nominees is positioned as bringing much-needed gaming industry expertise and accountability to the board, amid concerns over disappointing returns and strategic missteps.

Proxy firm Institutional Shareholder Services has weighed in on the vote by recommending that PENN Entertainment (PENN) shareholders vote for all three HG Vora nominees. ISS's report highlights the casino company's unsuccessful M&A activities, lack of board accountability, and insufficient gaming industry experience among current directors. ISS praised the qualifications of the HG Vora nominees, noting their industry expertise and potential to address the board’s deficiencies. ISS concluded that supporting the nominees is warranted, which mirrored a similar recent recommendation from proxy firm Egan-Jones.

PENN Entertainment defended itself last month against what it said was a campaign of misstatements and insinuations by HG Vora. "Our Board and management team are actively engaged with all shareholders and remain focused on executing on our strategy to create long-term shareholder value," read the letter to shareholders.

HG Vora Capital Management is a New York City-based investment management firm founded in 2009 by Parag Vora. The firm specializes in credit, value, and event-driven investments, with a focus on generating attractive risk-adjusted returns for institutional investors. HG Vora also has stakes in Boyd Gaming (BYD), Caesars Entertainment (CZR), and MGM Resorts International (MGM).

data.title: Nuclear Vision announces new private placement
data.description:
• Nuclear Vision (OTCQB:GDIGF) has announced a non-brokered private placement of up to 7.14M units of the Co. at a price of $0.28 per Unit for gross proceeds of up to $2M.
  • Each Unit will consist of one common share of the Co. and one common share purchase warrant.
  • Each Warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one Share at a price of $0.50 for a period of 24 months from the closing date of the Offering.
  • The proceeds will be used to advance the Co.'s assets in the Republic of Botswana, including but not limited to geophysical surveys, geological mapping, and permeability testing of the sandstone horizon at depth and its susceptibility to advanced Electric Pulse technologies.

data.title: Venus Concept prices $3.45M in direct offering, warrants; shares up over 29%
data.description:
• Venus Concept (NASDAQ:VERO) is raising $1.15M via a registered direct offering of 434,720 shares at $2.65 each.
  • In a concurrent private placement, VERO will issue unregistered short-term warrants to purchase 869,440 shares at the same $2.65 price.
  • The warrants are immediately exercisable and expire 18 months after the related resale registration becomes effective.
  • Gross proceeds could reach $3.45M if all warrants are exercised for cash.
  • Closing is expected around June 9, 2025. Net proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes.
  • VERO shares rise 29.3% on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: 'Moderately restrictive' monetary policy is still appropriate, Fed's Harker says
data.description:
The Federal Reserve's monetary policy is "moderately restrictive, and I think that's appropriate," Philadelphia Fed President Patrick Harker said in a broadcast interview on Friday.

That's where he expects it to stay for a while. Currently, the federal funds rate target range is at 4.25%-4.50%. The Federal Open Market Committee meets on June 17-18 to determine if the rate needs to be adjusted.

As the economy stands now, risks are to the upside, especially in terms of inflation, Harker said during an interview on CNBC. He cautions against cutting rates too soon, because "we are not where we want to be yet" in getting inflation to the Fed's 2% goal.

"Taking a pre-emptive action on hypotheticals, rather than what the hard data is telling us, is a mistake," he said.

He does see a path where the FOMC may lower rates later in the year. If tariffs' effects on inflation are resolved in coming months, if inflation continues to come down, "I could see a cut in the offing, but not right now."

The June meeting will be Harker's last at the central bank. He retires at the end of the month.

data.title: Microsoft marks new milestone as market cap hits $3.5T, holds slight edge over Nvidia
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) set a new milestone when its market capitalization reached $3.5T during trading on Friday, as the tech titan holds a slight lead over fellow Magnificent Seven member Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). 

Shares of Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) set a record high of $467.68 after increasing 0.8% by the time markets closed on Thursday. It was the first new all-time high recorded by the software giant since July 2024. Shares were up 0.6% by Friday afternoon trading, pushing its market cap to $3.5T. 

Meanwhile, Nvidia was up 1.3% by early afternoon trading with a market cap of $3.47T.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), the only other current member of the $3T-plus club, had edged up 1%, to give the iPhone maker a market cap of $3.02T.

Year-to-date, Microsoft has climbed 11% versus the Nasdaq (COMP:IND), which is up less than 1% in that time frame. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (BATS:IGV) has increased 5.5% in 2025.

On Friday, Bernstein Société Générale Group increased its price target on Microsoft to $540 from $520, while maintaining its Outperform rating. The financial services firm highlighted Microsoft's growth potential with Azure and its partnership with OpenAI.

data.title: Iron Mountain shares mark seven-session winning streak
data.description:
Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock moved 1.5% higher at $102.80 on Friday.

The real estate firm gained 3.6% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has fallen 2.7% so far this year, compared to a 1% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

IRM stock is up 6% over the past one month.

Iron Mountain raised its full-year guidance after turning in strong Q1 results as service revenue rose 8.8% Y/Y and storage services revenue gained 7.2%.

"Combined with IRM's raised FY2025 guidance, cheaper valuations, and the materialization of a floor during the recent meltdown, we believe its investment thesis remains compelling here," pointed out a Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, IRM has a Hold rating with a score of 3.1 out of 5. The company received D+ in the prospect of profitability, A in momentum, while it got a B+ growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, nine analysts gave IRM a Buy and above rating. No analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Strong Sell.

 
 
 
 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Hold.

data.title: American Vanguard lowers 2025 EBITDA and revenue targets while advancing cost containment and inventory management
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: American Vanguard Corporation (AVD) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Douglas A. Kaye opened the call noting that "the year 2024 was one of great change at American Vanguard and was then capped off by a prolonged financial close and audit that delayed our Form 10-K and consequently, the 10-Q." He emphasized that audited numbers were "substantially similar to the unaudited results" and highlighted progress in controllable areas: "our operating expenses dropped by $5 million and net trade working capital was reduced $86 million, both in comparison to last year."
  • Kaye reported, "channel inventories in the U.S. are at historic lows", and stated, "we are seeing a stronger second quarter and expect the remainder of 2025 will be solid." However, he also noted, "The first quarter of 2025 was somewhat weaker than we had initially anticipated" due to ongoing destocking, competitive program adjustments, absence of a previously canceled product, weakness in the Mexican agave market, and drought in Australia.
  • Metam sales were up 14% versus last year and Thimet sales rose 17%, reflecting positive product performance in targeted segments.
  • Kaye addressed guidance: "we are decreasing our full year adjusted EBITDA target range to $40 million to $44 million from $45 million to $52 million, and we are adjusting our revenue estimate to $535 million to $545 million."
  • CFO David T. Johnson acknowledged late filings and internal control matters at the Australian subsidiary, but stated, "as you may recall, we have published unaudited year-end financial information in March. Now that we have filed our financials, we have been able to report only minor changes from our prior [indiscernible]." Johnson reported, "Our first quarter 2025 revenue was $116 million, a decrease of 14% as compared to the first quarter of 2024."
  • Johnson added, "Gross profit margin declined to 26% during the quarter as compared to 31% last year."

Outlook

  • Management lowered full-year 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance to $40 million to $44 million and revenue guidance to $535 million to $545 million, citing a "weak first quarter and a market that is only beginning to recover."
  • Kaye said, "we are beginning to see early stages of a recovery, we do not want to forecast an overly optimistic outlook at this juncture."
  • Johnson expects CapEx to be in the $8 million to $9 million range for 2025 and "virtually all free cash flow to be allocated towards debt paydown."

Financial Results

  • First quarter 2025 revenue was $116 million, down 14% year-over-year, as reported by Johnson.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $3 million compared to $15.5 million in the prior year's first quarter, with the prior year benefitting from specific one-time items.
  • Gross profit margin declined to 26% from 31% in Q1 2024.
  • Operating expenses were down approximately $5 million year-over-year, excluding transformation expenses and a one-time benefit in Q1 2024.
  • Working capital ended $86 million lower than the prior year, and debt was approximately $20 million (14%) lower year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Benjamin David Klieve, Lake Street Capital, asked about the cadence of year-over-year top line performance and the impact of destocking. Kaye responded that "levels that we saw at the beginning of the year in the channel suggested that the inventories had been destocked. But it just continued through all the way to April... And then as we see now, we do see positive trends in May and June related to our sales."
  • Klieve requested detail on the first quarter EBITDA and revenue contributions from Dacthal. Kaye replied, "It's top line $6 million and margin 3.5." Klieve clarified this was on a gross margin basis, which Kaye confirmed.
  • Klieve asked about the balance of the portfolio. Kaye said it was "effectively flat with the highlights being on the other side, metam sodium and Thimet."
  • Klieve inquired about seasonality expectations. Kaye stated, "the second half of the year is always the strongest for us."
  • Klieve asked about implications of acreage shifts. Kaye explained, "Our portfolio does lend itself to corn acres with our corn soil insecticides."
  • Klieve queried cash tax expectations. Johnson said, "I think we're in the $4 million or $5 million range."
  • Wayne Pinsent, Gabelli Funds, asked about margin targets. Kaye stated, "nothing's changed in my outlook of the company and the goal of 15% EBITDA margin over the long term."
  • Pinsent asked about improvement in 2026. Kaye indicated, "We are projecting growth in 2026 in our 5-year plan ... as we rightsize and go through the transformation process on our commercial activities."
  • Rosemarie Morbelli, Gabelli Funds, requested details on pricing and generics. Kaye explained, "There was some very unusual activity that took place that's not normal for end of quarter-type situation" and described discounting due to low customer willingness to take on inventory.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on destocking trends, product-specific headwinds, and margin trajectory, reflecting a slightly negative to neutral tone and seeking clarity on recovery and confidence in future targets.
  • Management maintained a cautious but constructive tone in prepared remarks, acknowledging challenges but repeatedly stressing confidence in transformation efforts and long-term targets. Phrases like "we are beginning to see the benefits of our transformation efforts" were balanced by caution: "I don't want to sound too optimistic as the level of economic uncertainty remains extremely high."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone shifted towards more granular questions on headwinds and near-term recovery, while management became more explicit about cost containment and guidance adjustments, reflecting a more defensive but transparent posture.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance was lowered from $45 million-$52 million EBITDA and $565 million-$585 million revenue in Q4 2024 to $40 million-$44 million EBITDA and $535 million-$545 million revenue in Q1 2025.
  • Management focus shifted from emphasizing transformation and complexity reduction to spotlighting cost containment and working capital management.
  • Analysts' focus moved from structural changes and transformation costs to immediate product, margin, and destocking impacts.
  • Management’s confidence in achieving long-term margin targets remained, but short-term caution increased.
  • Key metrics including revenue, EBITDA, and gross margin all declined sequentially, with management attributing the drop primarily to destocking, product portfolio changes, and external market conditions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Ongoing destocking in the channel and delayed inventory replenishment constrained top-line performance.
  • Pricing pressure and aggressive competitor incentives impacted gross profit margin.
  • Specific geographic headwinds included weakness in the Mexican agave market and drought in Australia.
  • Internal control weaknesses at the Australian subsidiary and ERP implementation challenges were acknowledged by management, with remediation plans underway.
  • Management is prioritizing cost reduction, working capital efficiency, and flexible refinancing to address higher expected interest rates.
  • Tariff uncertainty and global trade dynamics continue to cloud the outlook, with management monitoring the situation closely.

Final Takeaway

American Vanguard’s first quarter of 2025 reflected ongoing challenges from market destocking, competitive pricing, and regional headwinds, prompting a reduction in full-year revenue and EBITDA guidance. Management underscored progress in cost containment and working capital improvements, as well as the resilience of key products like metam and Thimet. While near-term caution persists amid economic uncertainty and delayed recovery, the company remains focused on transformation, operational efficiency, and positioning for a cyclical upturn as inventory levels normalize and customer activity increases.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: S&P 500 pulls back from 6,000 points after crossing that level for first time since Feb
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Friday came off their session highs, after the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) earlier topped the 6,000 points level for the first time since late February. Sentiment was boosted by a strong May jobs report.

The S&P (SP500) was last +0.7% in afternoon trade, trimming gains of as much as +1.3% from a peak of 6,016.87 points. This was the first time the gauge hit the 6,000 mark since February 26. It last closed above that level on February 21.    

Meanwhile, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was last +0.9%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.7%. 

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields were substantially higher. The 10-year yield (US10Y) rose 11 basis points to 4.50%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 12 basis points to 4.05%.

Before the opening bell, May Nonfarm payrolls showed an increase of +139K vs. +126K consensus and +147K prior (revised from +177K), according to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.

Also, the unemployment rate came in unchanged at 4.2%, in line with consensus.

“Friday's jobs report likely doesn't change the Federal Reserve's expected pause on interest rates at their next meeting later this month," said Glen Smith, CIO at GDS Wealth Management.

"Friday's jobs report shows slowing but still solid growth, which may warrant a rate cut at some point towards the end of the year, at the Fed's November or December meetings. Right now, there is little reason for the Fed to cut rates in the very immediate term thanks to solid economic data,” he said. 

“With heightened recession fears, we expect the employment and inflation data to take center stage for the near-term as investors try to assess any potential economic fallout from the current state of trade uncertainty,” Smith added.

Wall Street closed in the red on Thursday as Tesla (TSLA) shaved off more than $100B in market capitalization amid a public verbal spat between U.S. President Donald Trump and CEO Elon Musk.

There were some indications of a potential thaw in tensions between Musk and Trump on Friday.

Turning to trade developments, Reuters reported that China had granted temporary export licenses for rare earth suppliers to the top three U.S. automakers, citing two sources familiar with the matter. China's strict export controls over rare earths has been a flashpoint in U.S. trade relations.  

Trump on Thursday said he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations.

“It's becoming clear that the rhetoric on tariffs is much tougher than the action. That is helping to improve market sentiment, as investors are getting more used to hard rhetoric on tariffs and are more accustomed to taking a wait and see approach,” GDS Wealth Management's Smith said.
